patches.summary: Map Amazon Ads env vars into OAuth refresh fields, persist local .env credentials, and add profile ID selection.
patches.reason: Amazon Ads requires both LWA refresh-token credentials and a selected advertising profile scope header on normal API calls; local .env loading and saving keeps setup usable without shell-sourcing secrets.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./internal/adsanalytics ./internal/client ./internal/cli ./internal/config passes; auth setup and doctor point to auth login --port 8085 with the localhost callback URL, verify-mode auth login emits the callback URL without opening a browser, auth login auto-selects a single returned advertising profile, provider 401 details surface for authorization-code and refresh-token failures, and incompatible/malformed seller-store data is reported in TACOS notes.

patches.summary: Add profitability, portfolio, campaign, product, hourly, keyword history, and spend optimization commands.
patches.reason: The Amazon Ads plan calls for a seller-focused novel command layer over COGS and exported report data.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./internal/adsanalytics ./internal/cli ./internal/config passes; automation --apply request shaping is covered with a local HTTP server plus ID, duplicate-suppression, max-change, max-bid, max-budget, empty-plan, --apply --dry-run preview, and PRINTING_PRESS_VERIFY short-circuit guardrail tests.

patches.summary: Add gzip-aware report loading plus a normalize-report command with optional local store import.
patches.reason: Amazon Ads downloaded reports are commonly gzip-compressed; the plan calls for report format normalization into local SQLite for downstream analytics.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./internal/adsanalytics ./internal/store ./internal/cli passes; reports wait exposes an async status poller for /v2/reports/{reportId}.

patches.summary: Add runtime MCP mirror coverage for Amazon Ads novel commands.
patches.reason: The static tools manifest only lists generated endpoint tools, while the MCP server exposes novel Cobra commands through the runtime cobratree mirror.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./internal/mcp ./internal/mcp/cobratree passes; the MCP server handles initialize and tools/list, exposes code-orchestration/framework/novel runtime tools, and marks automation tools destructive/open-world.

patches.summary: Rewrite the local generated module path to the public-library module path and remove generated binary payloads.
patches.reason: Published library CLIs use github.com/mvanhorn/printing-press-library/library/<category>/<slug> as their Go module path, and binary payloads under build/ are not publishable source artifacts.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./... passes after the import rewrite; no files remain under build/; simulated publish-package, binary-payload, and supply-chain verifiers pass.

patches.summary: Remove dead import guards, an unused accumulator, a duplicated lookup key, and recomputed values across novel paths.
patches.reason: Targeted cleanup pass: behavior-preserving, no flag/output changes. Removes the `var _ = strings.ReplaceAll` and `var _ = time.Now` compile guards (their packages are already used), drops `totalClicks` from KeywordCannibalization (collected and discarded), dedupes the `ad_group` lookup key in keyword snapshot ingestion, computes `splitCSV(keywords)` once in share-of-voice output, and reuses `sentCount` in applyAutomationMutation instead of recomputing it from `body any`.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./..., go vet ./..., go build ./cmd/amazon-ads-pp-cli ./cmd/amazon-ads-pp-mcp, and verify-skill (35 recipes, 0 findings) all pass.
